description: Redesign the side panel data flow so the background script is the single API surface for the side panel. The side panel never communicates with the MCP server directly — it only talks to the background script. The background script owns browser state (tab readiness, adapter injection) firsthand and caches server-owned state (tool config, browser tool policy, failed plugins, server version) received via sync.full and push notifications. Both caches persist to chrome.storage.session to survive MV3 service worker suspension. The MCP server remains the source of truth for registry/config data, but the background script mediates all access. For opentabs status, the MCP server queries the extension live for tab state instead of reading from its stale tabMapping cache. This eliminates the config.getState round-trip on startup, unblocks plugins.changed from sendTabSyncAll, and ensures state changes propagate instantly to the side panel.

userStories.title: Add server state cache with session storage persistence
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the background script to cache server-owned state with automatic persistence to chrome.storage.session so the cache survives MV3 service worker suspension.
acceptanceCriteria: A new module `platform/browser-extension/src/server-state-cache.ts` exports: getServerStateCache(), updateServerStateCache(partial), clearServerStateCache(), loadServerStateCacheFromSession()
The cache stores: plugins (with tool enabled states, source, sdkVersion, update info), failedPlugins, browserTools (with enabled state), serverVersion
In-memory cache is the primary read path (instant). Every mutation writes through to chrome.storage.session (fire-and-forget, not awaited)
Session storage writes are debounced (500ms) to coalesce rapid updates — in-memory cache updates immediately, only persistence is batched
loadServerStateCacheFromSession() reads from chrome.storage.session and populates the in-memory cache — called on service worker wake
clearServerStateCache() clears both in-memory cache AND chrome.storage.session, called from handleWsState when connected=false
Unit tests in server-state-cache.test.ts verify: get/update/clear behavior, debounced session storage writes, session storage load populates in-memory cache
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: New module at `platform/browser-extension/src/server-state-cache.ts`. The cache shape should align with ConfigStateResult from `platform/shared/src/wire-types.ts` (search for `ConfigStateResult`). Use a single `chrome.storage.session` key (e.g., `serverStateCache`) storing the serialized cache object. For write debouncing: use a module-level setTimeout that batches writes within 500ms — clear the timer on each update, only write when the timer fires. The in-memory cache is always immediately updated (no debounce on reads). Wire the `clearServerStateCache()` call into `handleWsState` in `platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts` — add it right after the existing `clearTabStateCache()` call on disconnect (around line 84). Import from the new module. Add unit tests following the patterns in existing test files (e.g., tab-state.test.ts). Mock chrome.storage.session in tests.

acceptanceCriteria: lastKnownState Map writes through to chrome.storage.session on every update (debounced 500ms)
A new loadLastKnownStateFromSession() export reads from chrome.storage.session and populates the in-memory Map
clearTabStateCache() clears both in-memory Map AND chrome.storage.session entry
In-memory Map is always the primary read path (instant reads, no async)
Existing functions (getLastKnownStates, computePluginTabState, notifyAffectedPlugins) continue to work unchanged

userStories.notes: In `platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.ts`, `lastKnownState` is a `Map<string, string>` (line 26) where values are serialized JSON of `{ state, tabs }`. Add write-through persistence: after every `lastKnownState.set()` call (in `computePluginTabState` and the `updateLastKnownState` function), schedule a debounced write to `chrome.storage.session` under key `lastKnownState`. Use the same debounce pattern as US-001 (500ms, module-level timer). Serialize the Map as a plain object (Object.fromEntries). Add `loadLastKnownStateFromSession()` that reads from session storage and populates the Map. Update `clearTabStateCache()` (line ~53) to also clear the session storage entry. Do NOT change the read paths — they remain synchronous reads from the in-memory Map. Add tests to the existing `platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.test.ts`.

userStories.title: Extend sync.full with all server-owned state and populate background caches
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want sync.full to carry failedPlugins, serverVersion, browserTools, and per-plugin source/sdkVersion/update so the background has complete data from a single message.
acceptanceCriteria: In the MCP server's sendSyncFull (extension-protocol.ts), the sync.full params now include: failedPlugins, serverVersion, browserTools (with enabled state), and per-plugin source, sdkVersion, update fields
A shared helper buildConfigStatePayload(state) extracts config.getState response building from handleConfigGetState — used by both sendSyncFull and plugins.changed senders
The extension's handleSyncFull (message-router.ts) extracts failedPlugins, browserTools, serverVersion from the enriched payload and calls updateServerStateCache()
handlePluginUpdate (message-router.ts) also calls updateServerStateCache() with the updated plugin data after storing meta and computing tab state

Existing message-router.test.ts tests updated to verify cache update in handleSyncFull and handlePluginUpdate

userStories.notes: TWO SIDES to this story. SERVER SIDE: In `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts`, extract the config.getState response building logic from `handleConfigGetState` (lines 273-311) into a shared helper `buildConfigStatePayload(state: ServerState)` that returns `{ plugins, failedPlugins, browserTools, serverVersion }`. Use it in handleConfigGetState (replace inline logic). In `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts`, `sendSyncFull` (line 54) currently sends `params: { plugins }`. Change to spread the buildConfigStatePayload result PLUS the existing per-plugin sourcePath/adapterHash/adapterFile fields. The sync.full plugins should have all config.getState fields (source, sdkVersion, update) merged with the existing sync-specific fields (sourcePath, adapterHash, adapterFile). Import buildConfigStatePayload. EXTENSION SIDE: In `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts`, `handleSyncFull` (line 235): after `storePluginsBatch`, extract `params.failedPlugins`, `params.browserTools`, `params.serverVersion` and the per-plugin server-owned fields, then call `updateServerStateCache()`. Also in `handlePluginUpdate` (line 326): after storePluginsBatch and computePluginTabState, call `updateServerStateCache()` to update the single plugin's data in the cache (merge the new plugin's tool enabled state, source, sdkVersion, update into the existing cache). This ensures the local plugins.changed sent by handlePluginUpdate triggers a getFullState that returns correct data.

userStories.title: Add bg:getFullState handler as the single API for side panel state
userStories.description: As a side panel developer, I want to call bg:getFullState to get all state from the background script so I never need to talk to the MCP server directly.
acceptanceCriteria: New bg:getFullState message type added to extension-messages.ts InternalMessage union

Handler in background-message-handlers.ts responds with: { connected, disconnectReason, plugins (merged from metaCache + lastKnownState + serverStateCache), failedPlugins, browserTools, serverVersion }
Each plugin has: name, version, displayName, urlPatterns, trustTier, iconSvg, iconInactiveSvg, tools (with enabled state), tabState, source, sdkVersion, update — merged from the three caches
If server state cache is empty (sync.full hasn't arrived), plugins from metaCache with default enabled=true and tabState from lastKnownState
On service worker wake (caches empty but wsConnected=true), loads from chrome.storage.session first via loadServerStateCacheFromSession() and loadLastKnownStateFromSession()
Message type added to EXTENSION_ONLY_TYPES set and backgroundHandlers map
Unit tests in background-message-handlers.test.ts for the new handler: full cache, empty cache, partial cache, session storage recovery
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: The handler merges three data sources: (1) `getAllPluginMeta()` from `platform/browser-extension/src/plugin-storage.ts` — plugin metadata with tools list (async, reads chrome.storage.local on first call), (2) `getLastKnownStates()` from `platform/browser-extension/src/tab-state.ts` — per-plugin tab state (Map<string, string> where values are serialized JSON `{ state, tabs }`), (3) `getServerStateCache()` from server-state-cache.ts — tool enabled states, source, sdkVersion, update, browserTools, failedPlugins, serverVersion. MERGE LOGIC: for each plugin name in metaCache, create a PluginState: take base fields from metaCache (name, version, displayName, urlPatterns, trustTier, icons), overlay tool enabled states from serverStateCache (if present, otherwise default all enabled=true), set tabState from lastKnownState (parse the JSON string, extract .state — default 'closed'), include source/sdkVersion/update from serverStateCache. WAKE DETECTION: check if lastKnownState is empty AND wsConnected is true — this indicates service worker woke from suspension. If so, await loadLastKnownStateFromSession() and loadServerStateCacheFromSession() before proceeding. The handler must be async because getAllPluginMeta is async and session storage loads are async. Add the type `BgGetFullStateMessage = { type: 'bg:getFullState' }` to extension-messages.ts. Include `connected` and `disconnectReason` from the module-level wsConnected and lastDisconnectReason variables. Add handler to backgroundHandlers map and EXTENSION_ONLY_TYPES.

userStories.title: Add server-request correlator and background mutation handlers
userStories.description: As a side panel developer, I want the background script to handle all mutation commands and correlate server responses so the side panel never sends JSON-RPC.
acceptanceCriteria: New module `platform/browser-extension/src/server-request.ts` exports: sendServerRequest(method, params), consumeServerResponse(data), rejectAllPendingServerRequests()
sendServerRequest generates a unique JSON-RPC id, sends via sendToServer, returns a Promise that resolves/rejects when the response arrives or times out (30s)
consumeServerResponse checks if a response matches a pending request — resolves/rejects the promise and returns true if consumed, false otherwise
rejectAllPendingServerRequests rejects all pending promises — called from handleWsState on disconnect
handleServerMessage in message-router.ts calls consumeServerResponse for responses before forwarding; only forwards to side panel if NOT consumed
New bg:* message handlers: bg:setToolEnabled, bg:setAllToolsEnabled, bg:setBrowserToolEnabled, bg:setAllBrowserToolsEnabled, bg:searchPlugins, bg:installPlugin, bg:removePlugin, bg:updatePlugin
Each handler relays via sendServerRequest and returns the result to the side panel callback
Tool toggle handlers optimistically update serverStateCache
All new message types added to extension-messages.ts, EXTENSION_ONLY_TYPES, backgroundHandlers map
handleWsState disconnect path calls rejectAllPendingServerRequests()
offscreen/index.ts switch statement: add no-op cases for all new bg:* types
Unit tests in server-request.test.ts and updated background-message-handlers.test.ts
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: SERVER-REQUEST MODULE: Create `platform/browser-extension/src/server-request.ts`. Use a Map<string | number, { resolve, reject, timerId }> for pending requests. sendServerRequest(method, params) generates an incrementing integer id, sends `{ jsonrpc: '2.0', method, params, id }` via sendToServer, registers the callback. consumeServerResponse(data) checks if `data.id` matches a pending entry — if so, resolve (data.result) or reject (data.error.message) and return true. rejectAllPendingServerRequests() iterates the map, rejects all with 'Server disconnected', clears all timeouts. BACKGROUND HANDLERS: In background-message-handlers.ts, add 8 handlers. Pattern for each: (1) extract params from message, (2) call sendServerRequest(method, params), (3) on resolve: sendResponse(result), (4) on reject: sendResponse({ error: message }). For tool toggles (bg:setToolEnabled, bg:setAllToolsEnabled): before calling sendServerRequest, optimistically call updateServerStateCache to update the tool enabled field. If sendServerRequest rejects, revert. For bg:setBrowserToolEnabled, bg:setAllBrowserToolsEnabled: same optimistic pattern. For bg:searchPlugins, bg:installPlugin, bg:removePlugin, bg:updatePlugin: pure relay, no optimistic update. WIRE UP: Add rejectAllPendingServerRequests() to handleWsState disconnect path (after clearServerStateCache). In handleServerMessage, change the isResponse block: call consumeServerResponse(message) first — if consumed, done. If not consumed, do NOT forward to side panel (no pending requests there). MESSAGE TYPES: Add BgSetToolEnabledMessage, BgSetAllToolsEnabledMessage, BgSetBrowserToolEnabledMessage, BgSetAllBrowserToolsEnabledMessage, BgSearchPluginsMessage, BgInstallPluginMessage, BgRemovePluginMessage, BgUpdatePluginMessage to extension-messages.ts. Add all to InternalMessage union. In offscreen/index.ts switch statement, add no-op cases for all new bg:* types.

userStories.title: Rewrite side panel bridge and App.tsx to use background-only communication
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to load instantly from the background script's local state with no MCP server round-trips.
acceptanceCriteria: bridge.ts: fetchConfigState, sendRequest, handleServerResponse, rejectAllPending, pendingRequests map, REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS — all removed
bridge.ts: New getFullState() sends bg:getFullState via chrome.runtime.sendMessage
bridge.ts: getConnectionState() removed — getFullState() includes connected/disconnectReason
bridge.ts: setToolEnabled, setAllToolsEnabled, setBrowserToolEnabled, setAllBrowserToolsEnabled send new bg:* messages
bridge.ts: searchPlugins, installPlugin, removePlugin, updatePlugin send new bg:* messages
App.tsx startup: calls getFullState() — single call, renders immediately from result
App.tsx: plugins.changed handler calls getFullState() to refresh
App.tsx: tab.stateChanged works as-is (push from background)
App.tsx: sp:connectionState on disconnect clears state; on reconnect calls getFullState()
App.tsx: pendingTabStates buffering removed
App.tsx: sp:serverMessage listener simplified — only handles notifications (no handleServerResponse)
BgSendMessage type removed from extension-messages.ts
bg:send handler removed from background-message-handlers.ts, backgroundHandlers map, and EXTENSION_ONLY_TYPES
offscreen/index.ts: remove bg:send no-op case
bridge.test.ts rewritten: remove sendRequest/handleServerResponse/rejectAllPending tests, add tests for getFullState and bg:* message functions
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: This is the largest story. BRIDGE.TS CHANGES: (1) Remove the entire JSON-RPC relay: sendRequest, handleServerResponse, rejectAllPending, pendingRequests Map, REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS, PendingRequest interface. (2) Remove getConnectionState — bg:getFullState includes connected/disconnectReason. (3) New getFullState(): `chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ type: 'bg:getFullState' }, callback)` wrapped in a Promise. Response shape: { connected, disconnectReason, plugins, failedPlugins, browserTools, serverVersion }. (4) Mutation functions: each sends the corresponding bg:* message and returns a Promise from the sendMessage callback. E.g., setToolEnabled(plugin, tool, enabled) sends { type: 'bg:setToolEnabled', plugin, tool, enabled } and resolves with the response. (5) sendConfirmationResponse unchanged (already uses sp:confirmationResponse directly). APP.TSX CHANGES: (1) Startup useEffect: call getFullState(). If connected, set all state. If not connected, set disconnectReason. Set loading=false. (2) Remove pendingTabStates ref entirely. (3) plugins.changed handler: call getFullState(). (4) sp:connectionState handler: on disconnect clear state; on reconnect call getFullState(). (5) Remove handleServerResponse from sp:serverMessage listener. (6) Remove the ws:message listener (was for sync.full — no longer needed since plugins.changed covers it). DEAD CODE REMOVAL: Remove BgSendMessage from extension-messages.ts. Remove handleBgSend from background-message-handlers.ts. Remove 'bg:send' from backgroundHandlers map and EXTENSION_ONLY_TYPES. Remove bg:send case from offscreen/index.ts switch statement. BRIDGE.TEST.TS: Remove all sendRequest/handleServerResponse/rejectAllPending tests. Add tests for getFullState (mocking chrome.runtime.sendMessage) and each mutation function (verify correct message type and params sent).

userStories.title: Unblock plugins.changed from sendTabSyncAll in handleSyncFull
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see plugin cards in the side panel immediately when the extension connects, without waiting for all isReady() probes to complete.
acceptanceCriteria: In handleSyncFull, plugins.changed notification sent to side panel immediately after storePluginsBatch, adapter injection, and server state cache update — BEFORE sendTabSyncAll
sendTabSyncAll runs after the notification (fire-and-forget, not awaited)
startReadinessPoll called after sendTabSyncAll completes (chained via .then())
Tab states stream to side panel progressively via tab.stateChanged as each probe completes
message-router.test.ts updated: verify plugins.changed is sent before sendTabSyncAll is awaited
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: In `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts`, `handleSyncFull` (line 235). Current order: (1) validate, dedupe, remove stale, (2) storePluginsBatch, (3) inject adapters, (4) await sendTabSyncAll(), (5) startReadinessPoll(), (6) forwardToSidePanel plugins.changed. New order: (1) validate, dedupe, remove stale, (2) storePluginsBatch, (3) inject adapters, (4) updateServerStateCache (already done from US-003), (5) forwardToSidePanel plugins.changed, (6) void sendTabSyncAll().then(() => startReadinessPoll()). The side panel receives plugins.changed, calls getFullState(), gets plugin cards from metaCache with tabState 'closed' (lastKnownState empty for fresh connections), renders immediately. Then sendTabSyncAll probes each plugin, and each completed probe sends tab.stateChanged to the side panel. Update message-router.test.ts: the test for handleSyncFull should verify that forwardToSidePanel is called BEFORE sendTabSyncAll. Use mock call order assertions.

Key risk areas: (1) bridge.test.ts — already rewritten in US-007, but verify no leftover imports. (2) known-methods.test.ts — if extension.getTabState was added to DISPATCH_METHODS (done in US-012), verify it's covered. (3) background-message-handlers.test.ts — new handlers added in US-005/US-006 need to be in the handler dispatch test. (4) message-router.test.ts — handleSyncFull ordering change (US-008), handleServerMessage response forwarding change (US-004/US-006). (5) extension-protocol.test.ts — sync.full payload changes (US-003), plugins.changed payload (US-004). (6) E2E tests — main risk is timing changes. The side panel loads faster now, so assertions with long timeouts are fine but short-timeout assertions that expected data to NOT be present yet may flip. Read each failing test, understand what changed, make minimal updates. Do NOT change the architecture to fix tests.

Key helpers: `openSidePanel(context)`, `waitForExtensionConnected(server)`, `openTestAppTab(context, url, server)`. userStories.notes: For tool toggle: use MCP client to call config.setToolEnabled → server sends enriched plugins.changed → background updates cache → side panel refreshes. For auth: testServer.setAuth(false) + app tab reload → isReady false → tab.stateChanged push. For tab close: close page → checkTabRemoved → notifyAffectedPlugins → tab.stateChanged. For browser tool: MCP client calls config.setBrowserToolEnabled. For reconnect: kill/restart server. NOTE: chrome.runtime.sendMessage may not reliably reach extension pages opened via URL in Playwright. If direct push assertions fail, use expect.poll that calls a known-working mechanism (e.g., evaluate extension APIs, or use the sp:getState diagnostic message). Read `e2e/CLAUDE.md` for conventions. Check existing tests in side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts and side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts to avoid duplication.

userStories.title: Add live tab state query from MCP server to extension for opentabs status
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want opentabs status to show live tab state by having the MCP server query the extension in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: New method extension.getTabState added to DISPATCH_METHODS in known-methods.ts
New handler in message-router.ts methodHandlers map: reads getLastKnownStates(), builds response, sends back via sendToServer with matching id
New queryExtension(state, method, params, timeoutMs) function in extension-protocol.ts — sends JSON-RPC request to extension WebSocket, returns Promise
MCP server /health endpoint calls queryExtension('extension.getTabState', {}, 2000) when extension connected, falls back to tabMapping if timeout/error
Existing tabMapping cache and tab.syncAll/tab.stateChanged flows preserved for tool dispatch
E2E test verifying /health returns accurate live tab state
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: EXTENSION SIDE: Add `extension.getTabState` to `DISPATCH_METHODS` in `platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts`. Add handler in `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts` methodHandlers Map: `['extension.getTabState', wrapSync('extension.getTabState', handleExtensionGetTabState)]`. The handler reads `getLastKnownStates()` from tab-state.ts, parses each serialized JSON value, builds a Map<pluginName, { state, tabs }>, and sends it back: `sendToServer({ jsonrpc: '2.0', result: { tabStates: Object.fromEntries(...) }, id })`. SERVER SIDE: In `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts`, add `queryExtension(state, method, params, timeoutMs)`: generates a unique id, sends `{ jsonrpc: '2.0', method, params, id }` to extensionWs, registers a callback in a pending-request Map, sets a timeout. When `handleExtensionMessage` receives a response (has id, no method), check if it matches a pending query — if so resolve. In `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts`, the /health handler builds pluginDetails with tabState from `state.tabMapping.get(p.name)?.state ?? 'closed'`. Change to: if extensionWs connected, try `queryExtension(state, 'extension.getTabState', {}, 2000)`, use the returned tabStates for each plugin. If query fails or times out, fall back to tabMapping. The queryExtension function needs to handle the case where the extension sends back a response — currently handleExtensionMessage treats all messages as either method calls or config/tab responses. Responses to queryExtension have an id that doesn't match any config/tab handler — they need to be consumed by the pending query map before falling through to the 'unknown response' path.
